Title: Recent Advances in the Metabolic Engineering of Lignan Biosynthesis Pathways for the Production of Transgenic Plant-Based Foods and Supplements
Journal: Journal of Agricultural and Food Chemistry

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream control point for redirecting lignan flux. In Forsythia, suppressing PLR by RNAi strongly increases pinoresinol glucoside and abolishes matairesinol, enabling introduction of exogenous sesamin production by stacking CYP81Q1. In Linum, PLR manipulation can tune flux toward secoisolariciresinol-derived lignans and hinokinin/podophyllotoxin-related branches; careful balancing is needed to avoid depletion of downstream endogenous lignans.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Controls conversion of pinoresinol toward downstream lignans; RNAi suppression causes pinoresinol accumulation and reduced downstream products such as matairesinol or hinokinin, while ABA-induced expression increases secoisolariciresinol diglucoside accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi suppression in Forsythia suspension cells and Linum hairy roots; metabolite profiling; expression analysis under ABA treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; red light; white fluorescent light; abscisic acid treatment; fluoridone treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In developing Linum usitatissimum responsive to abscisic acid; active in Forsythia suspension cells producing lignans
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome sequence of Linum usitatissimum mentioned; transcriptomic analyses mentioned for lignan-producing plants, but no accession provided
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to PIP family; acts enantiomer-specifically in many species. In Arabidopsis, AtPrR1 and AtPrR2 only reduce (-)-pinoresinol to (-)-lariciresinol, indicating functional diversification.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression blocks endogenous downstream lignans such as matairesinol and hinokinin; accumulated pinoresinol is largely glucosylated in Forsythia, limiting use by downstream heterologous enzymes
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Pinoresinol-lariciresinol reductase; PIP family benzylic ether reductase
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Forsythia spp.; Linum usitatissimum/Linum corymbulosum; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLR catalyzes a shared early step from pinoresinol toward secoisolariciresinol-derived lignans. 2. PLR RNAi in Forsythia abolished matairesinol and increased pinoresinol glucoside about 20-fold, enabling sesamin production when CYP81Q1 was coexpressed. 3. In Linum hairy roots, PLR RNAi reduced hinokinin, supporting PLR as a rate-limiting step for that branch. 4. ABA upregulated PLR expression together with secoisolariciresinol diglucoside accumulation in flax.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Pinoresinol; secoisolariciresinol diglucoside; matairesinol; hinokinin; sesamin (via engineered pathway)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Forsythia suspension cells; Linum hairy roots; developing flax tissues/seeds
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary/medicinal phytoestrogen-related compounds with reported antitumor, anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and plant ecological roles

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key heterologous engineering gene for introducing sesamin biosynthesis into non-sesame plants or cell cultures. Best deployed together with suppression of competing PLR and potentially UGT71A18 to preserve aglycone pinoresinol substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts (+)-pinoresinol to (+)-piperitol and then to (+)-sesamin by forming two methylenedioxy bridges
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables sesamin production; when expressed in PLR-RNAi Forsythia cells, confers de novo production of exogenous sesamin not made by native Forsythia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Molecular and biochemical characterization; stable transfection into Forsythia suspension cells; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light; red light; white fluorescent light increased sesamin production in engineered Forsythia cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in sesame seeds; heterologously active in Forsythia suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step from pinoresinol to sesamin
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Homologues detected in diverse Sesamum species; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First P450 enzyme characterized for lignan biosynthesis; CYP81Q clade catalyzes methylenedioxy bridge formation in furofuran lignans. Pinoresinol glucoside is not a substrate, limiting flux in Forsythia.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux limited by endogenous pinoresinol glucosylation in Forsythia; current production in CPi-Fk cells remains 10-20-fold lower than sesame seeds
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-introducing enzyme for sesamin production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP81Q1 is the first P450 identified for lignan biosynthesis. 2. It forms two methylenedioxy bridges to produce sesamin from pinoresinol via piperitol. 3. Stable expression with PLR-RNAi allowed transgenic Forsythia cells to produce sesamin, an exogenous lignan absent from native Forsythia. 4. Light further increased sesamin accumulation in engineered cells.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Sesame seeds; engineered Forsythia suspension cells
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary lignan with antioxidant, antihypertensive, hepatoprotective, and antitumor-associated activities

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Forsythia spp.; Linum spp.; Podophyllum spp.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential enzyme engineering target to alter enantiomeric pinoresinol formation and thus downstream lignan stereochemistry, but currently poorly characterized because no pinoresinol synthase has been identified.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in stereospecific coupling of two achiral E-coniferyl alcohol radicals to form pinoresinol stereoisomers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines stereospecific formation of basal lignan pinoresinol, the entry metabolite to major lignan pathways
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical functional assignment of dirigent protein in stereospecific coupling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A true pinoresinol synthase has not been characterized; DIR mediates stereoselective radical coupling rather than classic catalytic conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial coupling step of lignan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Insufficient molecular characterization limits direct deployment
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key entry stereochemical control step, rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DIR participates in stereospecific coupling of coniferyl alcohol to generate pinoresinol. 2. This step initiates major lignan biosynthesis. 3. The actual pinoresinol synthase remains unidentified, highlighting an unresolved entry step.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seeds, leaves, stems, fruits, flowers, and cultured cells depending on species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense, allelopathy/growth regulation, and dietary health benefits

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Forsythia spp.; Linum spp.; Podophyllum spp.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream flux gene to increase matairesinol-derived lignans if precursor supply is sufficient; supports pathway reconstruction toward arctigenin, hinokinin, and podophyllotoxin-related lignans.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides matairesinol, a central intermediate for multiple downstream lignans including arctigenin, hinokinin, and podophyllotoxin-related compounds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical characterization of SIRD activity; pathway inference from PLR-RNAi effects in Linum
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for multiple downstream target lignans
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Shared early pathway enzyme across diverse species including Forsythia, Linum, and Podophyllum
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct tradeoff reported; flux depends on upstream PLR availability
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Forsythia spp.; Linum spp.; Podophyllum spp.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SIRD converts secoisolariciresinol into matairesinol. 2. This establishes a shared early lignan pathway conserved across several genera. 3. SIRD-derived matairesinol feeds multiple specialized lignan branches.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary/medicinal lignan precursor with health-related bioactivities

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate engineering gene for producing arctigenin in lignan platforms once matairesinol supply is secured; relevant for extending Forsythia-type dibenzylbutyrolactone lignan production.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates a phenolic hydroxyl group of matairesinol-derived intermediate to form arctigenin
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recent identification and functional characterization as arctigenin synthase / matairesinol O-methyltransferase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target product or terminal tailoring step
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in Carthamus tinctorius, while arctigenin formation was discussed in Forsythia context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MOMT was identified as arctigenin synthase. 2. It catalyzes O-methylation in the matairesinol-to-arctigenin branch. 3. This discovery expands the set of enzymes available for downstream lignan engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate tailoring enzyme for reconstructing podophyllotoxin pathway in heterologous hosts; useful in combination with upstream matairesinol/pluviatolide pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Specifically methylates the 5-hydroxyl group of thujaplicatin, an intermediate of podophyllotoxin-related lignans
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to podophyllotoxin-related lignan formation through intermediate O-methylation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Isolation and biochemical characterization of enzyme specificity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts on thujaplicatin intermediate in podophyllotoxin-related branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. An Anthriscus OMT specifically methylates thujaplicatin at the 5-hydroxyl position. 2. This supports its role in podophyllotoxin-related lignan biosynthesis. 3. It represents one of the few identified enzymes in this otherwise incompletely resolved branch.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Relevant tissues of medicinal plants such as Podophyllum/Linum/Anthriscus systems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal lignans used for semisynthesis of anticancer drugs

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important branch enzyme for reconstructing podophyllotoxin biosynthesis in sustainable hosts. Can be stacked with upstream PLR, SIRD and later branch enzymes for podophyllotoxin production.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts (-)-matairesinol into (-)-pluviatolide through methylenedioxy bridge formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies a defined intermediate in the podophyllotoxin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Next-generation-sequencing-guided identification; molecular and biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic analysis of Podophyllum hexandrum; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to CYP719A clade, distinct from CYP81Q lignan methylenedioxy bridge enzymes, underscoring convergent molecular diversity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key characterized branch enzyme, rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP719A23 was identified from Podophyllum transcriptomics as a lignan biosynthetic P450. 2. It converts matairesinol to pluviatolide via methylenedioxy bridge formation. 3. It reveals that podophyllotoxin-pathway bridge formation is catalyzed by CYP719A rather than CYP81Q enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots/rhizomes or leaves depending on Podophyllum species
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor for anticancer semisynthetic drugs

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Equivalent engineering target to CYP719A23 for podophyllotoxin pathway reconstruction in sustainable platforms.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts (-)-matairesinol into (-)-pluviatolide through methylenedioxy bridge formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supplies a defined intermediate in the podophyllotoxin pathway
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Next-generation-sequencing-guided identification; molecular and biochemical characterization
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms pathway intermediate toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: From Podophyllum peltatum transcriptome-derived candidate enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomic analysis of Podophyllum peltatum; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homolog of CYP719A23; also in CYP719A clade rather than CYP81Q clade
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key characterized branch enzyme, rate limitation not established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP719A24 is a Podophyllum peltatum homolog catalyzing methylenedioxy bridge formation from matairesinol to pluviatolide. 2. It supports reconstruction of the podophyllotoxin branch. 3. Together with CYP719A23, it highlights lineage-specific enzyme solutions in lignan metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves and rhizomes of P. peltatum
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal precursor for anticancer semisynthetic drugs

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for producing stable glucosylated sesaminol supplements or for storage engineering; may enhance sequestration but could divert aglycone pools away from downstream modifications.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers one glucose from UDP-glucose to the 2-hydroxyl group of sesaminol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme identification and biochemical glucosylation assay
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: First lignan-glucosylating enzyme reported; homologs UGT71A8 and UGT71A10 share activity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May channel metabolites into glycosides rather than free aglycones
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT71A9 was the first identified lignan-glucosylating enzyme. 2. It glucosylates sesaminol at the 2-hydroxyl using UDP-glucose. 3. It defines glycosylation as an important late step in lignan storage and diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage/transport form of lignans in plants; dietary lignan source

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Homologous alternative for sesaminol glycoside production in engineering systems.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homolog of UGT71A9 sharing sesaminol glucosylation activity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert aglycone pool to glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT71A8 is a sesame homolog with sesaminol glucosylation activity. 2. It broadens evidence that lignan glycosylation is catalyzed by UGT family members.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Homologous alternative for sesaminol glycoside production in engineering systems.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homolog of UGT71A9 sharing sesaminol glucosylation activity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert aglycone pool to glycosides
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT71A10 is another sesame homolog with sesaminol glucosylation activity. 2. Together with UGT71A8/71A9 it supports conservation of lignan glucosylation function across Sesamum species.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering highly glycosylated, stable lignan storage products; relevant for supplement formulation but may reduce free aglycone availability.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates sesaminol 2-O-monoglucoside at the 6-position of the conjugated glucose to form sesaminol diglucoside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Specifically participates in sesaminol diglucoside biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts specifically on sesaminol monoglucoside rather than aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Further sequestration into glycosides may lower free lignan pools
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT94D1 catalyzes the second glucosylation step forming sesaminol diglucoside. 2. It demonstrates multistep sequential glycosylation in sesame lignan storage.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major anti-target to suppress when engineering sesamin or other aglycone-derived lignans in Forsythia, because glucosylated pinoresinol is not accepted by CYP81Q1. RNAi or editing could redirect flux to desired aglycone products.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glucosylates pinoresinol in a UDP-dependent manner
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes accumulation of pinoresinol glucosides; competes with engineered sesamin biosynthesis in Forsythia
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene identification and functional characterization; expression analysis under light in CPi-Fk cells
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with target-product branch; required for storage/modification of precursor
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Blue light and red light suppressed endogenous UGT71A18 expression in engineered Forsythia cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in Forsythia cells and contributes to pinoresinol glucoside accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: At least partly responsible for pinoresinol glucoside accumulation; around 90% of pinoresinol is glucosylated in Forsythia cells according to review summary
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong glucosylation limits aglycone availability for CYP81Q1 and lowers sesamin engineering yield
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key competing side-route step in engineered Forsythia
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT71A18 glucosylates pinoresinol and contributes to pinoresinol glucoside accumulation in Forsythia. 2. This side reaction competes with heterologous sesamin production because glucosylated pinoresinol is not a CYP81Q1 substrate. 3. Its expression is suppressed by blue or red light in engineered CPi-Fk cells, and targeted suppression is proposed to improve sesamin yield.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Heterologous glycosyltransferase useful for increasing seed lignans and proanthocyanidins and potentially improving pathogen resistance. Suitable for seed nutritional enhancement, but metabolic pleiotropy should be monitored.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Known to catalyze 7-O-glycosylation of flavonoids and anthocyanidins; in transgenic flax its expression increased lignan accumulation, probably via enhanced glycosylation/sequestration capacity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Stable transformation increased lignans and proanthocyanins in seeds and improved resistance to Fusarium pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable transgenic expression in Linum usitatissimum; metabolite analysis; pathogen resistance assays
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen infection context: Fusarium culmorum; Fusarium oxysporum
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native function described in Solanum sogarandinum
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review infers glycosylation may be limiting for lignan production in planta, likely through vacuolar sequestration analogous to flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Causes broad phenolic changes, increasing both lignans and proanthocyanins; potential compositional pleiotropy should be assessed
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Glycosylated phenolics; indirect increase in lignan accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Flavonoids/anthocyanidins canonically; likely broader phenolic substrates in transgenic context
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Suggests glycosylation can be limiting in planta
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SsUGT1 is a heterologous glycosyltransferase originally known for flavonoid/anthocyanidin 7-O-glycosylation. 2. Stable expression in flax increased seed lignans and proanthocyanins. 3. Transgenic flax also showed higher resistance to Fusarium pathogens, suggesting possible added agronomic value.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: L. usitatissimum PLR promoter containing abscisic acid-responsive elements
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: ABA treatment or engineering of ABA-responsive promoter/regulatory modules can be used to boost secoisolariciresinol diglucoside accumulation in developing flax tissues. Deployment should consider broader hormone effects on growth and stress physiology.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Abscisic acid treatment increased (+)-secoisolariciresinol diglucoside accumulation about 3-fold and upregulated PLR expression; fluoridone suppressed both
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hormone treatment and inhibitor treatment; PLR expression analysis; promoter cis-element and nuclear-protein binding evidence
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent hormonal controller that positively regulates lignan accumulation through PLR induction
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abscisic acid treatment; fluoridone inhibition of ABA synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Acts in developing Linum usitatissimum tissues
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Nuclear proteins binding ABA-responsive elements in the PLR promoter
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of secoisolariciresinol diglucoside via transcriptional activation of PLR
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This review does not identify a specific ABA-responsive TF gene; regulation is supported at the hormone/promoter level
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broader ABA effects on development and stress responses may accompany lignan enhancement
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hormone-response regulatory module, not a single gene identified
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PLR expression under ABA; fluoridone suppresses PLR expression indirectly by blocking ABA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus-associated regulatory response inferred for promoter binding proteins
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA enhances flax lignan accumulation, increasing secoisolariciresinol diglucoside around 3-fold. 2. ABA upregulates PLR expression, whereas fluoridone suppresses both PLR and lignan accumulation. 3. The PLR promoter contains ABA-responsive elements that bind nuclear proteins, supporting direct transcriptional regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Dietary phytoestrogen-related lignan with health-promoting potential
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Abscisic acid level; inhibition of ABA biosynthesis by fluoridone
